tools.description: Create a new GitHub issue for tracking bugs, feature requests, or tasks. Use this for actionable work items that need assignment, labeling, and status tracking. For reports, announcements, or status updates that don't require task tracking, use create_discussion instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be created. Labels ["test-reliability"] will be automatically added.

body.description: Detailed issue description in Markdown. Do NOT repeat the title as a heading since it already appears as the issue's h1. Include context, reproduction steps, or acceptance criteria as appropriate.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

labels.description: Labels to categorize the issue (e.g., 'bug', 'enhancement'). Labels must exist in the repository.

parent.description: Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Use '#aw_ID' in body text to reference other issues by their temporary_id; these are replaced with actual issue numbers after creation.

title.description: Concise issue title summarizing the bug, feature, or task. The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.

tools.name: create_issue

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

include_diff.description: Whether to include file diffs and stats in the response. Default is true.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

sha.description: Commit SHA, branch name, or tag name

tools.description: Get member usernames of a specific team in an organization. Limited to organizations accessible with current credentials

org.description: Organization login (owner) that contains the team.

tools.name: get_team_members

tools.description: Get details of the teams the user is a member of. Limited to organizations accessible with current credentials

user.description: Username to get teams for. If not provided, uses the authenticated user.

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

author.description: Author username or email address to filter commits by

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

sha.description: Commit SHA, branch or tag name to list commits of. If not provided, uses the default branch of the repository. If a commit SHA is provided, will list commits up to that SHA.

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

annotations.title: Get details for a single pull request

tools.description: Get information on a specific pull request in GitHub repository.

method.description:
Action to specify what pull request data needs to be retrieved from GitHub. 
Possible options: 
 1. get - Get details of a specific pull request.
 2. get_diff - Get the diff of a pull request.
 3. get_status - Get combined commit status of a head commit in a pull request.
 4. get_files - Get the list of files changed in a pull request. Use with pagination parameters to control the number of results returned.
 5. get_review_comments - Get review threads on a pull request. Each thread contains logically grouped review comments made on the same code location during pull request reviews. Returns threads with metadata (isResolved, isOutdated, isCollapsed) and their associated comments. Use cursor-based pagination (perPage, after) to control results.
 6. get_reviews - Get the reviews on a pull request. When asked for review comments, use get_review_comments method.
 7. get_comments - Get comments on a pull request. Use this if user doesn't specifically want review comments. Use with pagination parameters to control the number of results returned.
 8. get_check_runs - Get check runs for the head commit of a pull request. Check runs are the individual CI/CD jobs and checks that run on the PR.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: pull_request_read

query.description: Search query using GitHub's powerful code search syntax. Examples: 'content:Skill language:Java org:github', 'NOT is:archived language:Python OR language:go', 'repo:github/github-mcp-server'. Supports exact matching, language filters, path filters, and more.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only pull requests for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.description: Find GitHub repositories by name, description, readme, topics, or other metadata. Perfect for discovering projects, finding examples, or locating specific repositories across GitHub.

minimal_output.description: Return minimal repository information (default: true). When false, returns full GitHub API repository objects.

{"number":37864,"title":"Tracking: Known pre-existing CI test failures","body":"## Purpose

This issue tracks **known, pre-existing CI test failures** that are caused by infrastructure, service-side, or external dependency issues — not by code bugs in this repository. The automated [Analyze CI Test Failures](.github/workflows/fix-test-failures.md) workflow uses this issue to skip known failures and avoid filing duplicate issues.

## Known Failures

_No known CI (playback) failures at this time._

> **Note:** This list only tracks failures from **CI pipelines** (`ci.yml`, playback mode). Live-test pipeline failures (`tests.yml`) are excluded — the workflow filters them out by check-run name.

## Maintenance Guidelines

- **Adding entries:** When a CI failure is confirmed to be a pre-existing infrastructure/service issue (not a code bug), add it here with the package name, test pattern, error summary, root cause, and a tracking issue link.
- **Removing entries:** When the underlying issue is fixed and CI is green again, remove the entry and close the corresponding tracking issue.
- **Format:** Each entry must include the **package name** (as it appears in check-run names) so the workflow can match it.
- **Review cadence:** Review this list monthly to check if any known failures have been resolved.
","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/issues/37864","user":{"login":"deyaaeldeen","id":6074665,"profile_url":"https://github.com/deyaaeldeen","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/6074665?v=4"},"author_association":"MEMBER","labels":["test-reliability"],"reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-03-27T04:32:04Z","updated_at":"2026-03-27T16:33:26Z"}

[{"sha":"[REDACTED]","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/commit/[REDACTED]","commit":{"message":"feat(warp): add resolveImports for #platform import resolution (#37818)

## Motivation

Today, warp handles platform-specific code (browser vs Node vs React
Native) via **`polyfillSuffix`** — a filename-convention trick where the
compiler host secretly swaps `foo.ts` with `foo-browser.mts` at read
time. This works for emit, but it **breaks TypeScript's type-checking
model**:

- **TypeScript cannot see the real import graph.** The compiler thinks
it's reading `foo.ts` when it's actually compiling `foo-browser.mts`.
Type errors in one platform variant can be silently masked.
- **No IDE support.** Go-to-definition, find-references, and rename
don't understand the substitution — they always navigate to the default
file, not the platform-specific one.
- **The indirection is invisible.** There's nothing in the source code
that says "this module has platform variants." You have to know the
naming convention and check for sibling files.

This PR adds a **`resolveImports`** post-processing pipeline to warp
that enables packages to use [Node.js subpath
imports](https://nodejs.org/api/packages.html#subpath-imports)
(`#platform/*`) instead. This is the foundation for migrating away from
`polyfillSuffix` entirely.

## How it works

### Before (polyfillSuffix)

```
src/
  httpClient.ts              ← uses httpClientImpl directly
  httpClientImpl.ts          ← default implementation
  httpClientImpl-browser.mts ← browser variant (magic naming convention)
```

```yaml
# warp.config.yml
targets:
  - name: browser
    polyfillSuffix: -browser   # ← warp swaps file contents at compile time
```

TypeScript sees `httpClient.ts` for all targets. The browser variant is
invisible to the type system.

### After (subpath imports)

```
src/
  httpClient.ts              ← uses `import ... from "#platform/httpClientImpl"`
  httpClientImpl.ts          ← Node implementation (default)
  httpClientImpl-browser.mts ← browser implementation
```

```jsonc
// package.json
{
  "imports": {
    "#platform/*": {
      "browser": "./src/*-browser.mts",
      "react-native": "./src/*-browser.mts",
      "default": "./src/*.ts"
    }
  }
}
```

```jsonc
// src/tsconfig.browser.json
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "customConditions": ["browser"]  // ← TS resolves #platform/* to browser files
  }
}
```

Now TypeScript **resolves the platform choice itself** using standard
module resolution. The browser tsconfig sees browser types, the Node
tsconfig sees Node types. IDE navigation, rename, and find-references
all work correctly across platform boundaries.

The key shift: with `polyfillSuffix`, platform resolution is a
**build-tool trick** — only warp understands it. With `#platform/*`, it
becomes a **standard TypeScript feature**. Any TS-aware tool — `tsc`,
your IDE, API Extractor, ESLint — can independently resolve the correct
platform files per tsconfig. Each platform variant can be type-checked
**without running warp at all**.

Warp's new `resolveImports` step runs **after** compilation and rewrites
`#platform/*` specifiers in emitted `.js` and `.d.ts` files to concrete
relative paths, so published packages don't require runtime `imports`
resolution.

## What's in this PR

- **`resolveImports.ts`** — Node.js-spec-compliant subpath import
resolver:
- Implements `PACKAGE_IMPORTS_EXPORTS_RESOLVE`,
`PACKAGE_TARGET_RESOLVE`, and `PATTERN_KEY_COMPARE` from the [Node.js
spec](https://nodejs.org/api/packages.html#resolution-algorithm-specification)
- Supports exact keys, wildcard patterns (`#platform/*.js`), condition
objects, array fallbacks, and `null` targets
- Rewrites `import`/`export`/`require`/dynamic `import()` string
literals via TypeScript AST
- Uses WeakMap caching for pattern key sorting (called hundreds of times
per target with the same imports map)

- **`build.ts`** — Integrates `resolveImports` into the build pipeline:
- Automatically runs when `package.json` has an `imports` field (no
config needed)
- Builds the correct condition set per target (`customConditions` +
`import`/`require` + `default`)
  - Runs in parallel across targets

- **927 lines of tests** covering:
- Unit tests for `resolveSubpathImport` (exact keys, patterns,
conditions, arrays, null, edge cases)
- Unit tests for `resolveImportsInContent` (ESM import/export, CJS
require, dynamic import, `.d.ts` declarations)
  - Integration tests that run full warp builds and verify output files
- Regression tests for spec edge cases (empty pattern matches, pattern
key ordering)

## Part of a series

This is the first of 4 stacked PRs:

1. **This PR** — Add `resolveImports` to warp
2. `ts-http-runtime` platform refactor — migrate first package to
`#platform/*`
3. Remove `polyfillSuffix` — migrate all packages, delete old code
4. Simplify warp compiler — remove polyfill host, dedup logic, and other
dead code

---------

## Description

Adds `documentation/reviewer-agents.md` documenting the seven custom AI
reviewer agents available in this repository.

### Contents

- **Agent overview table** — name, trigger label, and focus area for
each agent
- **PR usage** — how to trigger reviews by adding labels, what to expect
(severity indicators, inline comments), and recommended label
combinations for common scenarios
- **VS Code local usage** — how to invoke agents in Copilot Chat using
`@agent-name` syntax, with examples for each agent
- **Configuration reference** — file structure for agent definitions
(`.github/agents/`), review guidelines (`.github/prompts/`), and CI
workflows (`.github/workflows/`), plus how to modify and recompile

### Agents Documented

| Agent | Label | Domain |
|-------|-------|--------|
| Archie | `architecture-review-needed` | Public API design |
| Dash | `performance-review-needed` | Runtime performance |
| Dexter | `dependency-review-needed` | Dependency management |
| Mgmt-review | `mgmt-review-needed` | Management SDK guidelines |
| Scribe | `docs-review-needed` | Documentation consistency |
| Sentinel | `security-review-needed` | Security vulnerabilities |
| Tester | `test-review-needed` | Test coverage and quality |

Co-authored-by: Deyaaeldeen Almahallawi <deyaa@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>","author":{"name":"Deyaa","email":"dealmaha@microsoft.com","date":"2026-03-27T18:58:01Z"},"committer":{"name":"GitHub","email":"noreply@github.com","date":"2026-03-27T18:58:01Z"}},"author":{"login":"deyaaeldeen","id":6074665,"profile_url":"https://github.com/deyaaeldeen","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/6074665?v=4"},"committer":{"login":"web-flow","id":19864447,"profile_url":"https://github.com/web-flow","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19864447?v=4"}}]

{"number":37864,"title":"Tracking: Known pre-existing CI test failures","body":"## Purpose

This issue tracks **known, pre-existing CI test failures** that are caused by infrastructure, service-side, or external dependency issues — not by code bugs in this repository. The automated [Analyze CI Test Failures](.github/workflows/fix-test-failures.md) workflow uses this issue to skip known failures and avoid filing duplicate issues.

## Known Failures

_No known CI (playback) failures at this time._

> **Note:** This list only tracks failures from **CI pipelines** (`ci.yml`, playback mode). Live-test pipeline failures (`tests.yml`) are excluded — the workflow filters them out by check-run name.

## Maintenance Guidelines

- **Adding entries:** When a CI failure is confirmed to be a pre-existing infrastructure/service issue (not a code bug), add it here with the package name, test pattern, error summary, root cause, and a tracking issue link.
- **Removing entries:** When the underlying issue is fixed and CI is green again, remove the entry and close the corresponding tracking issue.
- **Format:** Each entry must include the **package name** (as it appears in check-run names) so the workflow can match it.
- **Review cadence:** Review this list monthly to check if any known failures have been resolved.
